zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton於大約1325年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾。她和John More至少有1個女兒。她去世於1410年在她的家鄉享年86歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places called Hinton (for example, in Shropshire, Herefordshire, Gloucestershire, Somerset, Hampshire, and Northamptonshire). Some of the placenames, such as those in Northamptonshire, Shropshire, and Herefordshire, derive from Old English hīna, genitive plural form of hīwan ‘household, religious community’, + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’ (compare Hine as the first element). Others, such as those in Gloucestershire, Somerset, and Hampshire, derive from Old English hēan, dative form of hēah ‘high’ + tūn.

variants.name: Hine
meanings: English (Devon): occupational name from Middle English, Old English hīne ‘servant, member of a household’, also ‘farm laborer’ (such as a herdsman or shepherd). Originally a collective term for a body of servants, from an Old English plural noun, hīwan ‘household’, in early modern English it commonly acquired excrescent -d (see Hinde ). Evidence from Nottinghamshire shows that some bearers of the name were wealthy freemen, which suggests that their eponymous ancestors were not farmhands but senior members of a lord's household. From the late 15th century onward in western England, from south to north, a hine or hind also denoted a farm steward or manager, a sense that may be relevant to the surname in northwestern England, where hereditary naming had not been fully established by the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton was born about 1325, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with John More. She died in 1410, in her hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton est née environ 1325, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec John More. Elle est décédée en 1410, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton wurde um 1325 in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit John More mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1410 im Alter von 86 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton è nata il circa 1325 nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con John More. Morì nel 1410, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdo: イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hyntonは約1325年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた。彼女はJohn Moreとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1410年に故郷で86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton(은)는 대략 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John More(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1410년 그녀의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton nasceu aproximadamente 1325, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com John More. Ela faleceu em 1410, em sua cidade natal, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton родилась около 1325 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от John More. Она умерла в 1410 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Hynton nació aproximadamente 1325, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con John More. Murió en 1410, en su pueblo, a la edad de 86 años.
